title: Michael Sarnoski on Directing A Quiet Place: Day One
text: Director Michael Sarnoski made the leap from indie filmmaking his previous feature was the Nicolas Cage drama Pig to helm the blockbuster prequel A Quiet Place: Day One, which stars Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn. Sarnoski prioritizes character development regardless of budget. He explained that he usually starts with character and builds the world around that foundation. For this film, he focused on protagonist Sam, asking how New York and the alien invasion specifically impact her journey rather than simply including established franchise elements. Much of Day One features extended sequences without dialogue. Rather than altering his directorial approach, Sarnoski viewed this as an opportunity. He noted that the constraint made the scenes very intimate and forced the actors to communicate through understanding one another's intentions and physicality. The director received significant creative freedom from producer John Krasinski, who appreciated Pig. However, Sarnoski acknowledged that larger productions involve more stakeholders and require extensive communication. He plans to find balance with his next project a mid-range Robin Hood film with Hugh Jackman and Jodie Comer combining lessons from both small and large-scale productions.
